Obstetrics and Gynecology, Helsinki University Hospital, Finland; 8Faculty of Medicine and Health Technology, Tampere University, FinlandCancer -associated fibroblasts condition the ovarian cancer microenvironment and 
therapeutic responses to antibody -drug conjugates and immunotherapy
High -grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC) tumors are complex ecosystems where tumor–stromal–

roenvironment and 
therapeutic responses to antibody -drug conjugates and immunotherapy
High -grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC) tumors are complex ecosystems where tumor–stromal–
immune interactions shape therapeutic responses . Cancer -associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are 
major regulators of the tumor microenvironment, yet their spatially -segregated functional roles 
remain poorly understood . Using single -cell and spatial transcriptomics, along with patient -
derived multicellular cultures, we are investigatin g how CAF programs are organized at the 
tumor–stroma interface (TSI) and how they influence therapeutic responses .
PROJECT AIMSINTRODUCTION METHODOLOGY
